page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz, delivering 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the CPU demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% of tested servers with a stress-ng single-core score of 3113.77. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 49860.38 and a PassMark CPU score of 47478.25, both falling within the average range. Cryptographic capabilities are strong, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 38992.39, which ranks in the top 25%. Memory performance is also robust, with a PassMark memory score of 2912.7 placing it in the top 25%, while uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 99.54 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.g8ae.8xlarge is the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, which limits its utility for heavy machine learning training or high-IOPS local database workloads. However, the dedicated core allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced resource profile that avoids overpaying for excess memory or compute. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance alongside strong cryptographic and memory capabilities, both ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core compute and memory bandwidth fall within average ranges. Given its dedicated resource allocation and balanced hardware profile, this instance is suitable for enterprise web serving, cryptographic tasks, and general-purpose workloads that do not require local storage.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, top-tier single-core compute, and strong cryptographic performance for enterprise workloads.
